angle: advisor graph migration and routing parity

finding.angle: advisor graph migration and routing parity
finding.recommendation: Collapse the public advisor skill identity to one canonical graph skill, deep-loop-workflows, while preserving per-mode routing through a separate mode alias/discriminator layer. Do not keep the old five IDs as rankable advisor skills, and do not flatten all old IDs into one alias equality group without mode metadata. Rewrite skill-level expected outputs to deep-loop-workflows, keep routing-parity mode winners distinct, add deep-context/deep-improvement mode coverage, repoint finite inbound graph edges with duplicate edge contexts merged, and require post-scan rejectedEdges=0 plus UNKNOWN-TARGET grep.
key_tradeoffs: One canonical skill key satisfies folder-bound skill_id and two-skill architecture but requires fixture/schema updates for mode attribution.
Per-mode mode aliases preserve prompt-to-mode granularity but add a two-level skill-vs-mode concept to advisor code.
Naively preserving old skill IDs reduces fixture churn but conflicts with graph projection reality after old folders disappear.
Naively collapsing aliases to one equality group is simpler but makes deep-review, deep-research, deep-context, deep-improvement, and council indistinguishable for parity.
risks: A flat alias group can make distinct modes compare equal and hide routing regressions.
Hardcoded scorer boosts to deleted old IDs become inert because fusion ranks only projection.skills.
Duplicate inbound edges collapse under UNIQUE(source_id,target_id,edge_type), so contexts must be merged intentionally.
deep-context currently lacks alias and Candidate-3 coverage, so it can regress unless explicitly added as a mode.
Generated/static advisor graph JSON may remain stale unless rebuilt rather than hand-edited.
depends_on: Q-CMD 5-6 command surface and orchestration shape
Q-GOV 16 and 18 catalog/playbook and acceptance-gate decisions
open_questions: Can advisor response payloads add an explicit mode field, or must mode live in trace/attribution first?
Will category be constrained by downstream consumers; if yes, use workflow instead of autonomous-loop for deep-loop-workflows.
Are generated skill-graph JSON artifacts authoritative anywhere, or can they be regenerated exclusively after scan?
